params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

params.name: search
params.description: Search term to filter results

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by orchestration status

items.docs: Returns a collection of orchestration definitions within the tenant. Supports filtering by status, name, and trigger type.
Creates a new orchestration definition in the tenant. The orchestration is created in draft status and must be activated before it can be triggered.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Returns the details of a specific orchestration definition including its steps, triggers, configuration, and execution history summary.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Updates the definition of an existing orchestration. Only the fields provided in the request body are updated. Active orchestrations must be deactivated before modification.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Deletes an orchestration definition from the tenant. The orchestration must be inactive and have no pending executions before deletion.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Activates a draft or inactive orchestration, making it available for triggering and execution.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Deactivates an active orchestration, preventing new executions from being triggered. In-flight executions are allowed to complete.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by execution status

items.docs: Returns a collection of execution records for the specified orchestration, including completed, running, and failed executions.

info.name: Workday Extend Launch an orchestration execution

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Triggers a new execution of the specified orchestration. Supports passing input parameters that are mapped to the orchestration's defined inputs. This endpoint can be called from external systems to remotely trigger orchestration workflows.

info.name: Workday Extend Retrieve an orchestration execution

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

params.name: executionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration execution

items.docs: Returns the details of a specific orchestration execution including its status, step results, input and output data, and timing information.

info.name: Workday Extend Cancel an orchestration execution

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

params.name: executionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration execution

items.docs: Cancels a running orchestration execution. Steps that are currently in progress may complete before cancellation takes effect.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Returns the collection of steps defined within the specified orchestration, including their order, type, and configuration.

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Returns the collection of triggers configured for the specified orchestration, including event-based, scheduled, and API triggers.

info.name: Workday Extend Create an orchestration trigger

params.name: orchestrationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the orchestration

items.docs: Adds a new trigger to the specified orchestration. Triggers define how and when the orchestration is executed, including event-based triggers from business processes and scheduled triggers.
